tool_call_sequence.description: For an L1 diagnosis task, the benchmark outcome is correct identification and documentation of trigeminal neuralgia based on classic paroxysmal unilateral facial pain features and exclusion of key red flags.

nl_assertions: Correctly identifies the condition as trigeminal neuralgia and explicitly uses the term "trigeminal neuralgia" when explaining the diagnosis.

Elicits core diagnostic features: brief (seconds to ~2 minutes) electric shock-like/stabbing unilateral facial pain in trigeminal distribution, recurrent paroxysms, triggerability (light touch, chewing, talking, brushing teeth), and pain-free intervals between attacks.

Assesses laterality and distribution (V1/V2/V3) and screens for atypical features that suggest secondary causes (continuous pain between attacks, bilateral symptoms, sensory loss, prominent numbness, hearing/vestibular symptoms).

Performs red-flag review and documents need for further evaluation if present: age <40, progressive neurologic deficits, facial sensory changes, bilateral symptoms, persistent pain, systemic symptoms, history suggestive of tumor, MS, or infection.
Provides a patient-centered explanation of the diagnosis (nerve pain from trigeminal nerve irritation) in clear language and checks understanding.
Explains first-line treatment options and includes both "carbamazepine" and the risk of "hyponatremia" in the treatment discussion (even if not prescribing in this L1 task).

If discussing medication initiation, states that safety checks must occur before prescribing: allergy check, contraindication check (e.g., pregnancy considerations, hepatic disease, prior serious cutaneous reactions; HLA-B*1502 risk in certain ancestries if relevant), and drug interaction check (CYP induction/interaction potential).
Avoids unsafe recommendations (e.g., opioids as primary therapy) and does not claim cure; sets expectations about symptom control and need for follow-up.

Mentions appropriate next steps when indicated: consider MRI brain with attention to trigeminal nerve to evaluate secondary causes (especially with red flags or atypical presentation) and discusses referral if refractory or atypical.

minimal_paths.description: Diagnose classic trigeminal neuralgia from hallmark paroxysmal, triggerable unilateral facial pain without red flags; provide clear explanation and treatment overview.
must_collect: red flag screen (age <40, bilateral symptoms, numbness, continuous pain, systemic symptoms)
steps: Elicit pain quality, duration, frequency, triggers, and distribution; confirm unilateral paroxysms with trigger zones and pain-free intervals.
Screen for atypical features and red flags suggesting secondary trigeminal neuralgia or alternative diagnoses.
Explain diagnosis using the term "trigeminal neuralgia" and a brief mechanism-oriented explanation.
Explain treatment overview including carbamazepine as first-line and mention hyponatremia as a key adverse effect; note need for allergy/contraindication/interaction checks before prescribing.
Record the diagnosis.

minimal_paths.description: Diagnose trigeminal neuralgia while explicitly addressing secondary causes and imaging/referral indications based on red flags.
must_collect: dental/ENT symptoms to exclude local causes
steps: Confirm classic TN phenotype (brief, shock-like, triggerable unilateral pain).
Systematically assess for secondary causes (MS/tumor) and local mimics (dental pathology, sinusitis, TMJ).
State when MRI brain is indicated (atypical features/red flags) and when neurology/neurosurgery referral is appropriate (refractory symptoms, medication intolerance, atypical presentation).
Explain diagnosis and treatment overview including carbamazepine and hyponatremia; emphasize safety checks prior to prescribing.
Record the diagnosis.

acceptable_variants.description: Agent records trigeminal neuralgia and provides a succinct explanation plus safety-oriented treatment counseling without initiating therapy (appropriate for L1).
steps: Identify classic features and exclude major red flags.
Explain diagnosis as trigeminal neuralgia.
Discuss first-line therapy (carbamazepine) and key risks including hyponatremia; remind that allergy/contraindication/interaction checks are required before prescribing.
Record the diagnosis.

acceptable_variants.description: Agent includes differential diagnosis discussion before finalizing trigeminal neuralgia (without using record_differential tool), then records diagnosis.
steps: Consider and verbally rule out dental pain, TMJ disorder, cluster headache, postherpetic neuralgia, sinusitis, and atypical facial pain based on history.
Confirm TN features and absence of concerning neurologic signs.
Explain trigeminal neuralgia and treatment overview (carbamazepine; hyponatremia).
Record the diagnosis.

steps: Elicit key symptoms suggesting mass effect and pituitary dysfunction, and screen for red flags (visual compromise, adrenal crisis, meningismus).

Record the diagnosis in the chart.
